name: Ravine of La Hoz from Calomarde (SL-TE 40)
name_original: Barranco de La Hoz desde Calomarde (SL-TE 40)
region_intro: Hiking around Calomarde offers access to the dramatic landscapes of the Sierra de Albarracín in Teruel, Spain. The region is characterized by the Barranco de la Hoz, a gorge featuring rugged cliffs and unique rock formations. The Río Blanco meanders through the canyon, creating waterfalls and clear pools. Geological features like the Puente de la Toba (Tufa Bridge) and the imposing El Moriacho are prominent along the trails. Best hiking trails around Calomarde The most popular hiking route is Ravine of La Hoz from Calomarde (SL-TE 40), a 4.5 miles (7.2 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 20 minutes to complete. This route offers a comprehensive experience of the Barranco de la Hoz. Another top favourite among local hikers is Barranco de la Hoz Trail, an easy 2.9 miles (4.6 km) path. This trail features an intricate system of wooden bridges and elevated platforms navigating the gorge. Local hikers also love the Drowned Man Dam – Tufa Bridge on the Río Blanco loop from Calomarde, a 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes and unique geological formations, often completed in about 1 hour 20 minutes. Hiking around Calomarde is defined by dramatic gorges, river valleys, and unique geological formations. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy family walks to moderate excursions. The routes in Calomarde are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from nearly 100 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore Calomarde's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Calomarde?
A: Calomarde is renowned for its dramatic landscapes, particularly the &lt;strong&gt;Barranco de la Hoz&lt;/strong&gt; (also known as Cañón de los Arcos). You'll encounter rugged cliffs, unique rock formations, and lush vegetation. The Río Blanco meanders through the canyon, creating picturesque scenery with cascading waterfalls and crystal-clear pools. Geological features like the natural &lt;strong&gt;Puente de la Toba&lt;/strong&gt; (Tufa Bridge) and the imposing rocky mass of &lt;strong&gt;El Moriacho&lt;/strong&gt; are also prominent.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to visit on the trails around Calomarde?
A: Yes, a prominent attraction is the &lt;strong&gt;Cascada Batida&lt;/strong&gt;, also known as Cascada del Molino Viejo. This beautiful 20-meter high waterfall on the Río Blanco is easily accessible. It's particularly striking in spring when water levels are high, and can even freeze in winter, offering a unique spectacle. An easy 2.9-mile trail, the Batida Waterfall from Calomarde (PR-TE 2), leads directly to it.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of hiking trails in Calomarde?
A: Hiking options in Calomarde range from easy to moderate, catering to various fitness levels. There are 4 easy routes and 10 moderate routes available, with no difficult trails. This makes the area suitable for both casual walkers and those looking for a bit more challenge, including families with children.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking routes in Calomarde?
A: Yes, many trails in Calomarde are suitable for families. The overall difficulty ranges from easy to moderate, and the well-constructed paths, along with the constant presence of flowing water, make for pleasant walks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974394279/barranco-de-la-hoz-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de la Hoz Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.9-mile path that features wooden bridges and elevated platforms, which can be an exciting experience for children.

Q: What unique geological features can be found on the trails?
A: Calomarde's trails are rich in unique geological formations. You'll encounter the stunning &lt;strong&gt;Barranco de la Hoz&lt;/strong&gt; gorge with its sculpted cliffs. A distinctive feature is the &lt;strong&gt;Puente de la Toba&lt;/strong&gt; (Tufa Bridge), a natural calcareous formation that covers a section of the Río Blanco, creating a vaulted passage. Hikers will also see the imposing rocky mass of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4542067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Moriacho&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;strong&gt;Presa del Ahogado&lt;/strong&gt; (Drowned Man Dam).

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, Calomarde offers several circular routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31428845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Drowned Man Dam – Tufa Bridge on the Río Blanco loop from Calomarde&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 2.8-mile trail that takes you through riverine landscapes and past unique geological formations. There's also a longer version of this loop, approximately 10.2 miles, for those seeking a more extended circular walk.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Calomarde?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of nearly 4.8 stars from almost 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic gorges, the intricate system of wooden bridges and walkways along the Río Blanco, and the unique geological formations. The cool and pleasant canyon environment, even in summer, is also frequently highlighted as a positive aspect.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Calomarde?
A: The canyon's environment often remains cool and pleasant, even during summer months, making it a good option for hiking when other areas might be too hot. Spring is particularly striking due to higher water levels in the waterfalls, and winter can offer a unique spectacle if the Cascada Batida freezes.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can explore several interesting landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4542067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moricacho of the Gorge&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent natural monument. For cultural exploration, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/689101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albarracín Old Town&lt;/a&gt; and its &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6605680" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portal de Molina&lt;/a&gt; are nearby, offering a glimpse into the region's heritage.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Calomarde?
A: There are 14 hiking trails available in Calomarde, offering a variety of experiences. These include 4 easy routes and 10 moderate routes, ensuring options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: Is there a trail that features the famous wooden bridges and elevated platforms?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974394279/barranco-de-la-hoz-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de la Hoz Trail&lt;/a&gt; is famous for its intricate system of wooden bridges, metal walkways, stairs, and elevated platforms. These structures allow hikers to navigate the challenging terrain of the gorge and cross the Río Blanco, exploring otherwise inaccessible areas.
tips: It is an enormous cliff (or monolith) that rises above the Rio Blanco (Rio de la Fuente del Berro). You can get there walking from the road along a very colorful and picturesque path next to the river and on the other side the Alto de las Piedras.
Dam on the Blanco River that was dedicated to Joaquín Lozano (Molinero) and Pedro de Lahuerta who drowned in that place in 1876 after trying to cross it during a great storm

Natural bridge formed by the erosion of the river by dragging the carbonates dissolved by the waters, forming a passageway that then increases in size with the passage of water. On the tour you can go under and over the river in a very entertaining and colorful walk through walkways hanging from the rock that make for a very fun experience without technical difficulty.

Very cool hiking trail that runs along the Rio Blanco and at times even walking over it. It is very colorful and is very well cared for and maintained. It does not present technical difficulty although by sectors you have to be careful especially those who suffer from some vertigo. It is highly recommended and entertaining.

Ruins of an old water mill that marks the beginning of the Fuente del Berro River and that surrounds a curious legend.
Legend has it that it was formerly an inaccessible and forbidden place until one day a shepherd left his goats there while he hit the local pines with his stick. Suddenly the cavities of the mountain resounded, the cattle fled but the shepherd was chased by the very devil wrapped in flames. Despite this, the shepherd managed to flee by jumping into the river from the ravine, but the devil left his footprints there, which are still marked on the rock next to the mill.
Beautiful landscape formed by the erosion of the river over many years creating large caves that were once a refuge for shepherds and hunters.

Barranco Mingo Marco is a captivating natural wonder located in the Sierra de Albarracín, within the province of Teruel.
If you are an adventurous soul in search of natural beauty and historical intrigue, the Barranco Mingo Marco trail promises an unforgettable experience.

waypoints.name: Tufa Bridge on the Río Blanco
